name: Tanzania
avoid: March to May long rains can flood safari tracks and roughen Zanzibar seas
Bush-flight baggage is usually capped at 15kg in soft bags only
Stone Town and village areas are conservative away from resorts — pack modest layers
stopoverNote: Dar es Salaam (DAR) is the easiest gateway if you want Zanzibar first; Kilimanjaro (JRO) is better if you want to start directly on the northern safari circuit.

cities.name: Dar es Salaam
cities.notes: National Museum, Kivukoni waterfront, Coco Beach sunsets and the main ferry gateway to Zanzibar

cities.name: Zanzibar
cities.notes: Stone Town heritage, spice farms, Jozani red colobus forest and Indian Ocean beach time

cities.name: Serengeti
cities.notes: Seronera game drives, Great Migration timing, balloon safaris and classic tented camps

cities.name: Ngorongoro
cities.notes: Crater wildlife density, Maasai highlands lodges and one of East Africa's easiest Big Five days

cities.name: Arusha
cities.notes: Northern circuit launchpad with coffee estates, safari outfitters and Cultural Heritage Centre

cities.name: Kilimanjaro (base area)
cities.notes: Moshi, Materuni and Marangu foothills for waterfall walks, coffee farms and Kilimanjaro views

itinerary.sim: Vodacom and Airtel are the easiest airport/eSIM picks here; plan roughly ₹680–₹1,700 for starter data packs, with the best signal around Dar, Arusha and lodge zones in central Serengeti.

days.theme: Coastal arrival, city history, and an easy reset before the islands or safari loop

activities.name: National Museum and House of Culture ticket
Askari Monument, St. Joseph Cathedral and waterfront walk
Kivukoni Fish Market or Coco Beach sunset stop
Slipway waterfront browse for shops and sea views
Azam Marine ferry or Precision Air ticket for the Zanzibar leg

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Dar es Salaam, The Kilimanjaro

days.theme: Stone Town alleys, spice-scented markets, and a first Indian Ocean sunset

activities.name: Stone Town guided walking tour
Old Fort, House of Wonders exterior and Freddie Mercury Museum area walk
Old Slave Market museum and Anglican Cathedral visit
Prison Island boat trip to see giant tortoises
Forodhani Gardens street-food dinner crawl

days.theme: Jozani forest, spice-farm aromas, and the east-coast beaches

activities.name: Jozani Chwaka Bay National Park guided walk
Tangawizi or Kidichi Spice Farm half-day tour
Paje or Jambiani beach downtime
Shared taxi from Stone Town to the southeast coast and back
Pingwe beach stop by The Rock restaurant viewpoint

days.theme: Mnemba snorkeling, Nungwi sands, and a dhow-sail finale

activities.name: Mnemba Island half-day snorkeling trip
Nungwi beach and dhow-building yard walk
Mnarani Marine Turtles Conservation Pond entry
Sunset dhow cruise from Nungwi or Kendwa
North-coast taxi transfers for the day

hotels.name: DoubleTree Resort by Hilton Zanzibar - Nungwi

days.theme: Coffee-estate calm, gear checks, and a gentle northern-circuit start

activities.name: Arusha Coffee Lodge plantation and roasting tour
Cultural Heritage Centre art and curio browse
Arusha Declaration Museum or Natural History Museum stop
Shanga social enterprise workshop visit
Safari briefing and soft-duffel prep for bush flights

Ngorongoro.name: Ngorongoro
Ngorongoro.note: Best handled with a licensed 4x4 safari from Arusha or Karatu; crater descents run on NCAA permits and card payments.

days.theme: Crater-rim sunrise, dense wildlife sightings, and Maasai highlands scenery

activities.name: Ngorongoro Conservation Area entry fee for a non-resident adult
Crater descent service fee share in a 4x4 safari vehicle
Half-day crater game drive with driver-guide and picnic stop
Oldupai Gorge museum stop en route
Maasai boma cultural visit on the rim road

Serengeti.name: Serengeti
Serengeti.note: July to September is strongest for Mara River movement in the north, while January to February shifts the action toward the southern calving grounds.

days.theme: Bush-flight or overland arrival, Seronera plains, and the first big-cat scans

activities.name: Coastal Aviation or Auric Air bush flight into Seronera
Serengeti National Park non-resident entry fee for the first 24 hours
Afternoon shared 4x4 game drive in the Seronera Valley
Seronera Visitor Centre kopje walk and interpretive exhibits
Camp sundowner overlooking the plains

days.theme: Full wildlife day for migration herds, kopjes, and an optional balloon splurge

activities.name: Full-day game drive toward Moru Kopjes or seasonal migration sectors
Serengeti National Park entry fee for the second day
Optional hot-air balloon safari with bush breakfast
Picnic lunch setup at a designated safari site
Golden-hour predator drive back to camp

meals: Boma Grill at Four Seasons Safari Lodge Serengeti

days.theme: One last drive, wildlife from the lodge deck, and the fly-out toward Kilimanjaro or Zanzibar

activities.name: Morning game drive or river-track extension based on migration timing
Serengeti National Park entry fee for the third day
Lodge deck wildlife watching between drives
Coastal Aviation bush flight onward to Zanzibar or Kilimanjaro
Soft-bag porterage and airstrip transfer

days.theme: Moshi foothills, waterfall coffee culture, and a Kilimanjaro-view finale

activities.name: Materuni Waterfalls and coffee tour from Moshi
Kikuletwa Hot Springs day trip
Optional Kilimanjaro National Park day-visit permit via Marangu Gate
Moshi rooftop sundowner with mountain views
Private transfer between JRO, Moshi and day-trip sites

connections.method: Azam Marine fast ferry from Dar port or a short Precision Air flight if seas look rough
Precision Air or Air Tanzania flight to JRO/Arusha, then road transfer into town
Licensed 4x4 safari vehicle via Karatu with lodge or operator pickup
Continue overland by safari vehicle through Naabi Hill Gate or connect via a short bush-flight sector
Coastal Aviation or Auric Air bush flight to JRO, then a road transfer to Moshi
Precision Air or Air Tanzania domestic flight back to Dar for the India connection

extras: June to October is the safest all-round window for this route, with dry wildlife viewing and easier road conditions; July to September is strongest for Serengeti migration sightings, while January to February suits calving-season trips in the southern plains.
TANAPA and NCAA fees are normally card-only; keep TZS cash mainly for tips, market snacks and small taxi gaps.
Coastal Aviation and other bush carriers usually enforce a 15kg soft-bag rule, so avoid hard suitcases on safari sectors.
Zanzibar resort beaches are relaxed, but Stone Town and village areas reward modest shoulders-and-knees coverage out of respect for local norms.
Malaria prophylaxis is worth discussing before both mainland and Zanzibar stays, and yellow-fever proof may be checked if you transit through another endemic country.
If you only have one Serengeti base, stay in central Seronera for the easiest year-round game viewing and flight logistics.
